english: yis (yis) as I shall be with you Glr.; — the form byao (bya'o) frq. serves to express necessity: btsál-bar bya (btsal bar bya) I must seek Dzl.; esp. with a ne-gation: brjód-par mi byao (brjod par mi bya'o) they are by no means to be pronounced; the participles in the short forms of gtoń-byéd (gtong byed) and gtoń-byá (gtong bya) differ, in as much as the former is used in an active sense, e.g. one giving, a giver, the latter in a passive sense, one to be given; they may be formed of any verb. For spe-cific combinations, in which byéd-pa (byed pa) is differently to be translated, as dpe byéd-pa (dpe byed pa), yíd-la byéd-pa (yid la byed pa) etc., refer to these words. — 2. to say, to call, yet chiefly only in the pf. tense: źes byás-pa (zhes byas pa) Dzl. thus said, so called; sńar byás-pa bźin (sngar byas pa bzhin) according to what has been said before Dzl.; byas-kyań (byas kyang) though saying Pth. — and in the fut., which in that case, however, frq. stands for the pre-sent: (źes) byá-bai sgra byuń-no ((zhes) bya ba'i sgra byung ngo) a voice thus speaking was heard Glr.; dé-la dbyańs śes byao (de la dbyangs shes bya'o) these are called vowels Gram.; (źes-) byás-pa ((zhes) byas pa), or more frq. byá-ba (bya ba), the so called, being often joined to a name, that is mentioned for the first time, e.g. Anu, the so called, whilst we should say, a man, called Anu, or of the name of A.; byá-ba (bya ba) also implies: of the purport, to the effect, just as ćes-pa (ces pa) is also used: ‘tsol-źig’ byá-bai luń byuń-nas (‘tshol zhig’ bya ba'i lung byung nas) an order being given to make a search Glr. — 3. to go away, to disappear: byas soń (byas song) he disappeared Glr. — II. sbst. 1. byéd-pa (byed pa) and byéd-mkhan (byed mkhan), the person that does or has done a thing, the doer, performer etc.; author, bstan-bćos byéd-mkhan (bstan bcos byed mkhan) the author of the work Tar. — 2. byéd-pa (byed pa) the instrumentative case Gram. — 3. byéd-pa (byed pa) the doing, dealings, with noun in the instrum. case: dé-˳dra-ba mi-rigs-pa rgyál-pos byéd-pa (de 'dra ba mi rigs pa rgyal pos byed pa) such wrong being done by the king, such unjust dealings of the king Dzl.; in the genit. case: bló-yi byéd-pa dbyińs-su sbos (blo yi byed pa dbyings su sbos) hide the working of your understanding in the heavens, i.e. let it disappear in nothingness; effect, also with the noun in the genit. case, Wdn. — 4. byéd (-pa)-po (byed (-pa)-po), doer, accomplisher etc., mč́od-sbyin byéd-pa-por bos (mchod sbyin byed pa por bos) he invited him as sacri-ficing priest Tar.; ˳dúl-bar byéd-pa-po ('dul bar byed pa po) con-verter Tar.; bkra-śis spél-bai byéd-po (bkra shis spel ba'i byed po) aug-menter of eternal happiness (from a hymn); byéd-pa-po (byed pa po) instrumentative case Gram.; as the twelve byéd-pa-poi skye-mćed (byed pa po'i skye mched) I here cite the following from Wdk., without being able to offer an explanation: bdag (bdag), sems-ćan (sems can), srog (srog), ˳gró-ba ('gro ba), gsó-ba (gso ba), skyés-bu (skyes bu), gań-zág (gang zag), śed-can (shed can), śed-bdág (shed bdag), byéd-pa-po (byed pa po), tsór-ba-po (tshor ba po), śes-pa-po (shes pa po), mt́oń-ba-po (mthong ba po), where, by the by, it is to be observed, that thirteen are here enu-merated, byed-pa-po (byed pa po) being mentioned again with the rest (a want of accuracy, which is not unfrequently to be met with in the scientific works of the Tibetans). — 5. byá-ba (bya ba) q. v.
(also byiu (byi'u) q.v.) little bird; byeu-zúl byéd-pa (bye'u zul byed pa) v. zul (zul); byeu-la-ṕug (bye'u la phug) S.g., a medicinal herb Cs.

Lexx. and col.; foreign country; abroad, byes t́ag-riń-ba (byes thag ring ba) a far distant country Cs.; byés-su ˳gró-ba (byes su 'gro ba) to go abroad, to travel; byés-su ˳dég-pa (byes su 'deg pa) to remove, to emigrate Lex.; byés-nas sléb-pa (byes nas sleb pa) to come from abroad Lex.; byés-pa (byes pa) traveller, for-eigner, stranger; *lam-róg bés-pa yód-pa yin-te* (lam rog bes pa yod pa yin te) W. proceeding together as fellow-travellers.
Cs. rná-ba byó-ba (rna ba byo ba) to hear, hearken, listen.
animal, esp. quadruped; byol-sóń-bas glén-pa (byol song bas glen pa) more stupid than a brute Mil.; byol-sóń rgyál-po (byol song rgyal po) the lion Mil.
n. of a lunar mansion, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar) 2.

1. sbst., n. of a small rodent, living under ground (not mole Cs., but rather suslik, earless marmot Sch.); brá-ṕu-se (bra phu se) Ld. a similar animal (= pra-li? (pra li?)); bra-mkár (bra mkhar), bra-tsań (bra tshang) Cs., burrow of it; bra-brún (bra brun)
